title: Manager (Volunteer Management)
description:
The Manager oversees the recruitment, development, engagement and governance of volunteers to strengthen the organisation’s programmes and services. This role is pivotal in building a sustainable volunteer ecosystem through effective deployment, robust governance and meaningful communications that foster inclusive community participation.

Key Responsibilities

Volunteer Recruitment, Development & Engagement

  • Plan and execute volunteer recruitment strategies to achieve optimal support for seniors across programmes.
  • Design and curate volunteer opportunities that attract, engage and retain individuals and groups, ensuring roles are impactful and sustainable.
  • Oversee volunteer deployment to ensure effective and sustainable support for elderly.
  • Implement structured onboarding, training and orientation programmes to prepare volunteers for service.
  • Drive volunteer recognition and appreciation initiatives to strengthen retention.


Governance & Policy

  • Develop, review and implement volunteer management policies and SOPs in line with NCSS Volunteer Management Toolkit and AIC guidelines.
  • Implement and manage a volunteer management system to monitor participation, deployment and performance effectively.
  • Ensure accurate documentation and timely reporting to meet organisational and stakeholder requirements. 

Communications & Advocacy

  • Work closely with Corporate Communications to design campaigns and narratives that promote inclusive volunteerism in healthcare.
  • Position the organisation as a leader in volunteer engagement by promoting the value and impact of volunteer contributions.
  • Strengthen collaborations with corporate partners, community and grassroots organisations to broaden and diversify volunteer pools.

Job Requirements

  • Bachelor’s degree not limited to eldercare or social services with at least 5 years of relevant experience.
  • Demonstrated experience in working with volunteers, corporate partners, community groups and foundations.
  • Analytical ability to draw insights, consider multiple perspectives and adapt to evolving needs.
  • Self-motivated, resilient and able to work independently while contributing effectively as a team player.
  • Multilingual skills are an advantage.
  • Flexibility to work evenings, Saturdays, and/or Sundays when required.

Competencies:

Must-have:

  • Collaborative Practices Across Disciplines and Sectors - Level 4
  • Volunteer Programme Management - Level 4
  • Volunteer Programme Evaluation - Level 4
  • Volunteer Retention and Engagement - Level 4
  • Volunteer Recruitment - Level 4
  • Stakeholder Management - Level 4
  • Diversity Awareness and Management - Level 4

Good-to-have:

  • Trends Evaluation and Application - Level 4
  • Diversity Awareness and Management - Level 4

Critical Core Skills:

  • Communication - Intermediate
  • Influence - Intermediate
  • Collaboration - Intermediate
  • Adaptability - Intermediate
  • Decision Making - Intermediate

*Please refer to Technical Skills and Competency (TSCs) and Critical Core Skills (CCSs) in Skills Framework for Social Service Industry via this link SSG | Social Service (skillsfuture.gov.sg)
